title: Psephellus albovii
intro: Psephellus albovii, also known as Albov's golden daisy, is a perennial plant species belonging to the Asteraceae family. Traditionally, Psephellus albovii has been used in folk medicine for its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which have been associated with various health benefits, including the relief of respiratory and gastrointestinal issues. The bioactive constituents of Psephellus albovii include sesquiterpene lactones, flavonoids, and phenolic acids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. In traditional herbal medicine, Psephellus albovii is often used to make teas, infusions, and tinctures, which are used to treat various health conditions, although more research is needed to confirm its efficacy and safety.
taxonomy: Psephellus albovii is a species of flowering plant in the family Asteraceae. This plant is classified within the genus Psephellus, which is a group of plants commonly known as scree asters. The family Asteraceae is a large and diverse group of flowering plants that includes many species of asters, daisies, and sunflowers. Psephellus albovii is native to the Caucasus region. The classification of Psephellus albovii is further defined by its unique characteristics and physical traits.
